summary: Alfred demonstrates strong system comprehensibility with 6 documented ADRs, a clean 5-crate architecture enforced by Rust's compiler, comprehensive inline documentation, and 183 passing unit tests. The main risk is complexity concentration in app.rs (7,449 lines). The plugin-first design with Lisp extensibility is well-executed and thoroughly tested.

recommendations.title: Extract app.rs into sub-modules
recommendations.description: Split crates/alfred-tui/src/app.rs (7,449 lines) into focused modules: operator_pending.rs, visual_mode.rs, colon_commands.rs, search.rs, macro_replay.rs. The handle_key_event function handles 13 InputState variants in a single match.

recommendations.title: Group EditorState fields into sub-structs
recommendations.description: EditorState has 30+ fields covering search, macros, visual mode, jumps, and change lists. Grouping related fields into SearchState, MacroState, VisualState, and JumpState sub-structs improves readability without changing behavior.

recommendations.title: Split bridge.rs into category modules
recommendations.description: The bridge module registers 20+ primitives in one 2,472-line file. Split into bridge/buffer.rs, bridge/cursor.rs, bridge/keymap.rs, bridge/hooks.rs, bridge/theme.rs for easier navigation and maintenance.

recommendations.title: Add property-based tests for pure functions
recommendations.description: CLAUDE.md specifies property-based testing as the default strategy, but no proptest/quickcheck tests exist. Cursor movement and buffer operations are pure functions with well-defined domains -- ideal candidates for property testing.

recommendations.title: Add backward search (?pattern)
recommendations.description: Forward search (/) is implemented but backward search (?) is missing. This is a high-usage vim feature that shares most of the infrastructure with forward search.
